# SC-07: Permission Management

**Track:** Security Controls (`SC`) · **Priority:** MUST (mandatory) · **Status:** draft

Enforce least-privilege access on all agent operations during implementation. Define and apply the permission boundary governing which files, APIs, databases, and network resources the agent may access. Any access attempt outside the defined boundary must be blocked and logged.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 3 | Coding & Implementation | Mandatory |

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Automated (policy-enforced) |
| **Agent does** | Operate strictly within defined permission boundary; log all access attempts. |
| **Human does** | Define and maintain permission policies per agent trust tier; review access violation logs. |

## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Permission enforcement log | evidence | Log of all access requests, granted permissions, and blocked attempts during the implementation session. | 7 years (DORA) |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_change |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | — |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | block |
| **Escalate to** | Security Architect |

Out-of-boundary access attempts are blocked immediately and logged as security events.

## Dependencies

None

## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 9(4)(c) | Access and identity management policies must govern all ICT systems including agents |
| EU AI Act | Art. 15(4) | Protection against unauthorised third-party manipulation through access controls |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Security · **Owner role:** Security Architect
